svg.text: REGULATORY ANALYSIS — BRAZIL'S CRYPTO AML FRAMEWORK: THREE CRITICAL GAPS
Case AML-IR-2026-007 · BCB Resolutions 519/520/521 · IN RFB 2.291/2025 (DeCripto) · Lei 14.478/2022 · Effective: February 2026
Framework Flow Diagram — Reporting Obligations vs. Analytical Gaps
• PIX shows as bank transfer only
Ahead of many EU MiCA jurisdictions
Brazil: ahead of most LATAM peers
close most of the analytical gap.
COAF should issue guidance on P2P

g.text: Multiple PIX → immediate crypto → ext. wallet · ACAMS 3.2.1
Multiple txs just below DeCripto reporting limit · ACAMS 3.1.4
PSAV → unhosted wallet → unknown dest. · BCB Res. 521
BRL → USDT → intl transfer to bypass FX · BCB Res. 521
Brazilians using non-BCB-authorized PSAVs · BCB Res. 519
Funds from unrelated CPFs → crypto purchase · ACAMS 6.1
Txs structured to trigger DeCripto, avoid STR · ACAMS 7.3

svg.text: What I Do as AML Analyst Today

g.text: STR to COAF — include full on-chain data:
origin address + TXID + blockchain + analytics output
even if government systems don't integrate
crypto purchase → external wallet = EDD trigger
DeCripto-reportable tx also has red flags
EDD for R$50K+ with no verifiable source

svg.text: AML Red Flags — Brazilian Crypto Compliance Context

g.text: • COAF STR gap: on-chain data in STR cannot be investigated without blockchain analytics — compliance theater risk (ACAMS 7.3)
• DeCripto/STR parallel systems: same transaction reported twice to different agencies in different formats with no cross-referencing
• P2P via PIX: largest unmonitored crypto channel in Brazil — no KYC, no STR, no DeCripto, no reporting entity (FATF R.15)
• Structuring below R$35K DeCripto threshold — multiple transactions just under limit (ACAMS 3.1.4 · Lei 9.613/1998)
• Self-hosted wallet transfers without owner identification — BCB Res. 521 gap in enforcement
Frameworks: ACAMS 7.3 · FATF R.15/R.20/R.29 · Lei 9.613/1998 · BCB Res. 519/520/521 · IN RFB 2.291/2025

svg.text: Author: Phelipe Agnelli — AML & Blockchain Forensics · Sources: BCB Resolutions 519/520/521 · IN RFB 2.291/2025 · Lei 14.478/2022 · Lei 9.613/1998 · PL 5256/2025 · COAF · FATF · AML-IR-2026-007
